strengths: 15+ years of full-stack experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with recent TypeScript/Node.js product work
Strong software-craftsmanship background (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, testing workshops and open-source test tooling)
Current hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling experience at re:cinq, matching the AI agent development requirement
concerns: No evidence of Nest.js, Kotlin, or Python; Java exposure is brief, dated (2016-2018), and peripheral to the required multi-language proficiency
Testing expertise centers on Selenium and D-language tooling rather than the required Jest/Vitest/Playwright, and frontend history is Ember-heavy with only one role citing React
Significant career investment in the niche DLang ecosystem plus several short (~1-1.5 year) tenures raise questions about stack alignment and retention in a TypeScript-first team

key_factors.factor: Limited React and modern JS testing-framework evidence
Niche DLang focus and short recent tenures

response.justification: While the candidate clearly exceeds the 6-year experience bar and brings genuine craftsmanship and recent AI-agent work, the fit against this specific stack is weaker than the resume length suggests. Nest.js, Kotlin, and Python are absent entirely; Java is minimal and stale; the required Vitest/Jest/Playwright toolchain is unevidenced, with testing credentials rooted in Selenium and D-language libraries; and frontend depth skews to Ember rather than React. Combined with a career pattern of niche-ecosystem investment and several sub-two-year stints, there is real risk the candidate's strengths lie adjacent to, rather than inside, the role's core TypeScript/Nest/React requirements.
